jokes.text: Season five Carrie Bradshaw, like, super professional and polished, but also like, chill and flirty in an impossibly expensive sort of way
jokes.setup: Alexis describing her outfit goal for college
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top, impossibly specific reference to Sex and the City for a community college
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Alexis's delusional glamour expectations for mundane situations

jokes.text: You do know that this is your first day at Elmdale College, and not Vogue, right?
jokes.setup: Alexis's elaborate fashion planning
jokes.punchline: David's dry reality check about community college vs. fashion magazine
jokes.explanation: Clean straight-man response with perfect economy and character voice

jokes.text: Okay, I think that says more about Jocelyn as a teacher than it does her students
jokes.setup: Jocelyn claiming only her top students go to Elmdale
jokes.punchline: Implying that if Elmdale is for her 'top' students, Jocelyn must be a terrible teacher
jokes.explanation: Sharp logic-based burn that reveals character dynamics

jokes.text: Yeah, and you're already wearing your little gloves, though
jokes.setup: Johnny asking them to take out garbage
jokes.punchline: Alexis pointing out Johnny's already wearing cleaning gloves as if that settles the matter
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's privileged logic - if dad has gloves on, clearly he should do it

jokes.text: This is the same college where Malala gave that devastating commencement speech, right? / I don't know, David, maybe he did
jokes.setup: David asking about Malala (female Nobel Prize winner) speaking at the college
jokes.punchline: Alexis responding 'maybe he did' - wrong gender and casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Alexis's oblivious privilege and David's pretentious name-dropping in one exchange

jokes.text: It's a pregnancy test. / Ewww! What?!
jokes.setup: David asks why Johnny is waving a thermometer
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted reaction to learning it's actually a pregnancy test
jokes.explanation: Standard shock reaction but well-delivered for the revelation

jokes.text: Well, it's a smiley face, so I'm assuming it's a positive result. / Well, that's presumptuous
jokes.setup: Johnny interpreting the pregnancy test result
jokes.punchline: Moira's characteristically pretentious response to the word 'positive'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary moment - treating 'positive' as presumptuous

jokes.text: Oh, are you serious? I was ready to stop after one!
jokes.setup: Family discussing the pregnancy test
jokes.punchline: Johnny's immediate assumption they'll need to support multiple children
jokes.explanation: Shows Johnny's practical panic about their financial situation

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure she already knows it's a mistake!
jokes.setup: Parents saying someone needs to talk to Alexis
jokes.punchline: David's brutally honest assessment that Alexis knows the pregnancy is a mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - simultaneously supportive and brutally honest

jokes.text: Yes, I'll tell her she has no right to lay this financial and psychological burden on us!
jokes.setup: Agreeing to talk to Alexis supportively
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning to tell her off about burdening them
jokes.explanation: Classic Johnny - trying to be supportive but immediately showing his real priorities

jokes.text: Now she has to move into one of those homes for unwed mothers! / Oh my God, imagine? / I'm sure there are plenty of them on this side of the tracks. Let the nuns take care of the little stranger!
jokes.setup: Discussing what to do about Alexis's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatically outdated solution involving unwed mothers' homes and nuns
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - dramatically outdated references delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: I am not in a place right now to be emotionally available to a baby
jokes.setup: Family crisis about supporting Alexis
jokes.punchline: David's therapeutic language to avoid responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - using therapy speak to be selfish in the most articulate way possible

jokes.text: Okay, I'm sorry I'm not a condom
jokes.setup: Johnny blaming David for not looking out for Alexis better
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic response about his contraceptive responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant one-liner that perfectly deflects Johnny's unfair blame with dark humor and perfect economy

jokes.text: Because it requires a steady hand, and I'm going through a lot right now
jokes.setup: Patrick asking why David can't apply his own eye serum
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic excuse about needing steady hands due to emotional turmoil
jokes.explanation: Shows David's tendency to dramatize and make others cater to his emotional state

jokes.text: Yeah, we don't sell that. / Bath salts? / We actually do sell those
jokes.setup: Customer asking for something they assume the store doesn't carry
jokes.punchline: The mundane reveal that they were asking about bath salts
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection about what the customer was asking for

jokes.text: Unclear. Unclear on whether I'm gonna make it through or not
jokes.setup: Patrick asking if David will make it through this situation
jokes.punchline: David's overly dramatic uncertainty about surviving Alexis's pregnancy
jokes.explanation: David's characteristic overdramatizing of situations

jokes.text: You mean, the ones on top of your head? / And there they are
jokes.setup: Moira looking for her sunglasses
jokes.punchline: They were on her head the whole time
jokes.explanation: Classic absent-minded physical comedy, elevated slightly by Moira's dignity

jokes.text: Well, you've cracked me open
jokes.setup: Jocelyn saying she doesn't want to pry
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic response using food metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Moira - using dramatic food metaphor for emotional vulnerability

jokes.text: To allow an unscheduled conception to occur!
jokes.setup: Moira expressing outrage about the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Treating pregnancy like a missed calendar appointment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira-ism turning pregnancy into bureaucratic failure

jokes.text: Moira, I'm the one who's pregnant
jokes.setup: Entire scene assuming Alexis is pregnant
jokes.punchline: The reveal that it's actually Jocelyn
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist that recontextualizes the entire episode so far

jokes.text: Oh Jocelyn, Roland's not the father?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn saying she hasn't told Roland yet
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate assumption of infidelity
jokes.explanation: Shows Moira's tendency toward dramatic assumptions

jokes.text: Miracles don't take reservations
jokes.setup: Discussing unexpected pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Moira's pseudo-profound statement mixing theology and hospitality
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira wisdom that sounds profound but is actually nonsense

jokes.text: I knew this was gonna happen! I knew you were gonna get way too involved in this
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to be supportive about what he thinks is her pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Alexis treating his concern as overinvolvement in her college decision
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - she has no idea what he's really talking about

jokes.text: If I don't like it, I'll just give it up, and move on!
jokes.setup: Johnny's concern about her handling this responsibility
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually talking about giving up college, while he thinks she means a baby
jokes.explanation: Dark dramatic irony - sounds horrifying in pregnancy context, casual for college

jokes.text: David and I were just in the back doing some inventory. / Yeah. / Hmm, yeah
jokes.setup: Patrick and David clearly having been intimate
jokes.punchline: Stevie's knowing pause and skeptical response
jokes.explanation: Standard 'caught in the act' awkwardness with minimal creativity

jokes.text: David, did you give me a hickey?! / Okay no, I didn't, I gave you a-a half hickey, because we haven't had the time or the privacy for me to give you a full hickey!
jokes.setup: Patrick discovering a hickey on his neck
jokes.punchline: David's defensive explanation about hickey completeness due to circumstances
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation showing their relationship desperation through hickey logistics

jokes.text: If you give me $50 bucks I'll stand outside, and tell people you're on lunch. / I'll give you 50 cents to stand outside and never come back!
jokes.setup: Stevie offering to help them get privacy
jokes.punchline: David's counter-offer that's both cheaper and more insulting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and negotiation humor with character-specific rudeness

jokes.text: I'll give you 50 cents to stand outside and never come back!
jokes.setup: Stevie's $50 offer
jokes.punchline: David's counter-offer of almost nothing to get rid of her
jokes.explanation: Good comeback showing David's frustration and cheapness

jokes.text: Well, if you must know, work's been really stressful, so I'm going to take a little me time at a spa in Elmdale
jokes.setup: David questioning Stevie's generous apartment offer
jokes.punchline: Stevie's obviously fake cover story delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Setup for the later reveal, but works as awkward lying in the moment

jokes.text: And you might want to add a line item for prophylactics
jokes.setup: Johnny making financial plans for a baby
jokes.punchline: Moira's formal word choice for condoms as a budget item
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary turning practical advice into formal business speak

jokes.text: So... I'm guessing the quad is just like, on the other side of the building? / The office is in there, make a right. / A 100%
jokes.setup: Alexis expecting an elaborate campus based on the brochure
jokes.punchline: The reality is just a simple hallway direction, which she accepts with false enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Shows the gap between Alexis's expectations and reality through campus tour deflation

jokes.text: Yeah, we couldn't afford in house photography, so we just used stock images. See? Watermark there. This is actually a still from 'Dead Poets Society.'
jokes.setup: Alexis questioning the brochure's accuracy
jokes.punchline: The casual admission that they used a famous movie still for their college brochure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail about low-budget college marketing using Hollywood imagery

jokes.text: Oh, and like, um, an agenda. / Yeah, it's off by a day. So... I wouldn't use it
jokes.setup: Student listing the perks of the college
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercutting the agenda by admitting it's wrong
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the college's incompetence through mundane failure

jokes.text: Oh, and like, um, an agenda. / Yeah, it's off by a day. / So... I wouldn't use it.
jokes.setup: Listing what students get
jokes.punchline: Even their free agenda is wrong and unusable
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of incompetence, efficient delivery

jokes.text: Okay, then I guess I would just like my money back, then. / Again, we don't give refunds
jokes.setup: Alexis trying different ways to get her money back
jokes.punchline: Student's patient repetition that they don't give refunds
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of bureaucratic inflexibility

jokes.text: That's actually how I keep my side hustle going, part time DJ. Here's my card, in case you have any problems, or you wanna go on a date, or something
jokes.setup: Student explaining online courses
jokes.punchline: Casually hitting on Alexis while handing out DJ business cards
jokes.explanation: Shows the low-budget nature of the college through multi-tasking staff

jokes.text: Here's my card, in case you have any problems, or you wanna go on a date, or something.
jokes.setup: Offering help
jokes.punchline: Unprofessionally hitting on student
jokes.explanation: Completes the unprofessional college picture

jokes.text: I noticed the line is a couple inches above where the whiskey starts. / I am stunned by your generosity
jokes.setup: Stevie saying she marked her alcohol
jokes.punchline: David noting the mark is above the alcohol level, Patrick's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Stevie's suspicious generosity

jokes.text: Hey, pony! Are you ready to go?
jokes.setup: Someone arriving to pick up Stevie
jokes.punchline: Using 'pony' as a pet name
jokes.explanation: Sets up David's discomfort with the pet name

jokes.text: Yeah, just galloping around the woods, huh? / Yeah, with your 'pony.'
jokes.setup: Jake calling Stevie 'pony' and mentioning woods
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic emphasis on the nickname with horse imagery
jokes.explanation: David's jealous sarcasm using horse metaphors for their relationship

jokes.text: So you offering your apartment had nothing to do with any guilt you felt about harboring a little secret?
jokes.setup: David realizing Stevie's generosity was suspicious
jokes.punchline: Calling out her guilt about hiding her relationship
jokes.explanation: David's detective work revealing Stevie's motivation

jokes.text: Look out!
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions being hungry
jokes.punchline: His self-aware warning about his hangry behavior
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor about Johnny knowing his own hangry tendencies. Self-aware but not particularly witty.

jokes.text: Besides wanting to keep me as your husband? Am I right, Johnny? / Oh, I need to be a part of this
jokes.setup: Roland making inappropriate comment about his wife's cooking
jokes.punchline: Johnny's uncomfortable desire to join the conversation
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor from Roland with Johnny's awkward participation

jokes.text: Rollie, how are you not getting this? / I'm pregnant!
jokes.setup: Roland completely missing all the pregnancy hints
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn finally having to state it directly
jokes.explanation: Standard oblivious husband comedy

jokes.text: I'm pregnant! We're having a baby. / Twyla, I'm gonna have the bottomless French Onion soup, and maybe you might wanna drop a little calamari on the table
jokes.setup: Jocelyn's big pregnancy announcement
jokes.punchline: Roland immediately pivoting to ordering food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland move - completely missing the emotional moment for food

jokes.text: Champagne for everybody, on me! / I can't drink, Rollie. / Oh right, you're driving
jokes.setup: Roland celebrating pregnancy with champagne
jokes.punchline: Completely missing that pregnant women can't drink, assuming she's driving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland obliviousness - missing obvious pregnancy implications

jokes.text: I couldn't be happier that this is happening to you and your family!
jokes.setup: Moira offering congratulations to Jocelyn and Roland
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing that she's happy it's happening to THEM (not her)
jokes.explanation: Perfectly backhanded Moira congratulation emphasizing her relief

jokes.text: Given that we only have the apartment for one night. Maybe it's best if we... lock that box back up for now?
jokes.setup: David and Patrick dealing with relationship drama
jokes.punchline: Patrick's metaphor for shelving their issues to focus on romance
jokes.explanation: Good euphemistic metaphor for putting aside relationship issues. Patrick's practical approach to compartmentalizing problems.

jokes.text: You know what, we didn't even get into your history... / Lock it up, David. Lock it up
jokes.setup: David starting to probe Patrick's dating history
jokes.punchline: Patrick firmly shutting down that conversation
jokes.explanation: Patrick learning to manage David's tendency to overshare and probe

jokes.text: Dad's trying to get me to drop out of college. / No, that's not what was happening! I-I thought we were talking about something else. / What else? / Oh, he thought you were pregnant
jokes.setup: Alexis misunderstanding Johnny's earlier conversation
jokes.punchline: The reveal of the massive miscommunication
jokes.explanation: Great payoff revealing how the whole family miscommunicated

jokes.text: David, Alexis isn't... / No, it's okay. I mean, have your fun now David, because when the twins arrive, I'm gonna really need your help. / Um, that's a hard pass
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to correct David about the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: David's immediate rejection of helping with hypothetical twins
jokes.explanation: Classic David selfishness in rejecting family responsibility

jokes.text: Okay, but just picture them as like, tiny little roommates, whose tiny little poops you get to clean up!
jokes.setup: Trying to convince David to help with babies
jokes.punchline: Alexis's tone-deaf sales pitch emphasizing the worst parts
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Alexis - trying to make baby care sound appealing but highlighting the gross parts
